ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for angel hair pasta with scallops and truffle oil. FOODON_00002473 (pasta food product) is the closest defensible parent class.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC (Pasta, cooked) is the closest parent. No specific code exists for angel hair pasta with scallops and truffle oil as a composite dish.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 229048009 (Pasta dish) is the closest parent concept. No specific term for this composite Italian seafood pasta dish exists in SNOMED CT.

serving.natural_language_portion: One restaurant serving of chilled angel hair pasta with Hokkaido scallops and truffle oil, approximately 320 g, as served at Ricciotti The Riverwalk, Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Cooked pasta (spaghetti/capellini, enriched, boiled) and sea scallops (raw) composite estimate; truffle oil contribution modelled from USDA FoodData Central olive oil and truffle oil entries
nutrient_source.organization: USDA FoodData Central — Foundation Foods and SR Legacy
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct entry exists for this composite restaurant dish. Values are estimated by combining USDA FoodData Central data for cooked capellini/angel hair pasta (FDC ID 168936), raw sea scallops (FDC ID 175177), dried seaweed (FDC ID 168456), and truffle oil (modelled as olive oil with truffle flavouring, FDC ID 171413). Proportions estimated as approximately 55% pasta, 35% scallops, 5% seaweed flakes, 5% truffle oil by weight. The dish is served chilled. All values are per 100 g of the composite dish as plated.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson FS et al.; capellini / thin pasta analogous estimate
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for chilled angel hair pasta with scallops and truffle oil. GI estimated at 45 based on the University of Sydney GI Database entry for thin pasta / capellini (GI range 32–52 for al dente or chilled pasta; chilling increases resistant starch content, lowering GI). The dominant glycemic contributor is the pasta component (~55% of dish weight). Scallops, seaweed, and truffle oil contribute negligible glycemic load. GL per serving calculated as (45 × 56 g carbohydrate per 320 g serving) / 100 = 25.2. Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC, Buyken AE, Goletzke J. International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values 2021. Am J Clin Nutr. 2021.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content from scallops and seasoning
hypertension.guidance: Each 320 g restaurant serving provides an estimated 672 mg sodium; individuals with hypertension should request reduced seasoning and monitor portion size to keep daily sodium intake below 2000 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load from refined pasta carbohydrates
type2_diabetes.guidance: The estimated glycemic load of approximately 25 per serving is moderate; people with Type 2 Diabetes should consider a half-portion, pair the dish with additional non-starchy vegetables, and monitor postprandial blood glucose, as refined pasta can raise blood glucose even when served chilled.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat with heart-healthy monounsaturated fat from truffle oil
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The dish is relatively low in saturated fat (~2.2 g per serving) and provides monounsaturated fats from truffle oil and omega-3 fatty acids from Hokkaido scallops, which are broadly consistent with a cardioprotective dietary pattern; however, sodium should be monitored.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Capelli d'Angelo Capesante e Tartufo is a refined Italian pasta dish served chilled, consisting of angel hair pasta (capellini) dressed with truffle oil, topped with premium Hokkaido scallops and garnished with seaweed flakes. The dish is a signature offering at Ricciotti The Riverwalk, a well-regarded Italian restaurant in Singapore. Angel hair pasta (capellini) is one of the thinnest pasta formats, with a delicate, silky texture that absorbs dressings readily. When served chilled, the pasta develops a slightly firmer bite and a higher resistant starch content, which moderates its glycemic impact compared to hot-served pasta. Hokkaido scallops are prized for their natural sweetness, tender texture, and clean oceanic flavour. Truffle oil imparts an earthy, umami-rich aroma that elevates the dish to a luxury dining experience. Seaweed flakes add a subtle marine salinity and provide trace minerals. Nutritionally, the dish is a moderate-energy, moderate-carbohydrate meal with a meaningful protein contribution from the scallops. The fat profile is dominated by monounsaturated fats from the truffle oil, with low saturated fat content. The dish contains mollusc allergens (scallops) and gluten (wheat pasta). From a clinical perspective, the moderate glycemic load and sodium content make portion awareness important for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or hypertension. The dish is not suitable for vegetarians or vegans due to the scallops.

confidence_reason: No direct nutritional entry exists for this specific restaurant composite dish. Values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data for cooked capellini, raw sea scallops, dried seaweed, and truffle oil using plausible weight proportions. GI is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database using chilled thin pasta as the closest analogous food. Confidence is medium rather than low because the component sources are high-quality approved sources and the estimation methodology is transparent and defensible.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data (cooked capellini FDC 168936, sea scallops FDC 175177, dried seaweed FDC 168456, olive/truffle oil FDC 171413) using approximate weight proportions of 55% pasta, 35% scallops, 5% seaweed, 5% truffle oil. These proportions are estimates and actual restaurant plating may vary. 2. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central for the composite dish, and not reliably estimable from component sources. 3. trans_fat_g is 0.0; confirmed negligible/trace in all components per USDA FoodData Central — confirmed zero by component source data. 4. GI is estimated at 45 based on chilled thin pasta analogous entry from the University of Sydney GI Database; chilling pasta increases resistant starch and lowers GI relative to hot-served pasta. 5. The dish is served at Ricciotti The Riverwalk, Singapore, at SGD 45.78. 6. Cuisine tags from the input source included 'Pizza' and 'Chicken Dish' which are not relevant to this specific dish and have been excluded from the record. 7. This is an Italian cuisine dish; multilingual naming rules for Italian cuisine do not require Singaporean language set (Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) — those rules apply to Singaporean dishes. No mandatory non-English local names are required for Italian cuisine under the Veritas v2 multilingual matrix.
